'''Sam Luckley''' (born 29 November 1995) is a Scottish rugby league footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}} for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]]. He notably played for {{rlnt|Scotland}} at the 2022 World Cup.

== Early life ==
Luckley was born in [[Kirkcaldy]], Scotland and grew up in [[North Shields]], England.<ref>{{cite web|date=20 September 2022|title=Luckley joins Robins|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/3072/luckley-joins-robins|work=Super League|access-date=9 August 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|last=Appleyard|first=Joe|date=2 June 2023|title=Sam Luckley hoping for hometown glory with Hull KR at beloved St James' Park|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/sam-luckley-hull-kr-newcastle-8489109|work=Hull Daily Mail|access-date=9 August 2025}}</ref> He is a lifelong supporter of [[Newcastle United]].

== Playing career ==
Luckley previously played for Salford Red Devils in the super league and in the past has represented [[Newcastle Thunder]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]]. He has also spent time on loan from Salford at the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

In 2021 he made his [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] début in the [[Super League]] against the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP3543323 Salford Red Devils v Warrington Wolves]</ref>

On 12 August 2023, Luckley featured for [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]] during the [[2023 Challenge Cup Final|Challenge Cup Final]], to which they lost 17-16 in [[golden point|extra time]] to [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

Luckley played a total of 27 games for Hull Kingston Rovers in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fourth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in the clubs semi-final loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/67008107|title=Relive Wigan's thumping win v Hull KR to head to Grand Final|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 12 October 2024, Luckley played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[2024 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Wigan. On 7 June 2025, he played in the 2025 Challenge Cup final as Hull KR beat Warrington 8-6 to win their first major trophy in 40 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 18 September 2025, Luckley played in Hull Kingston Rovers 2025 Victory over Warrington in the last game of the regular season which saw the club lift the League Leaders Shield <ref>https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c39rzxxpyjeo</ref>
On 9 October 2025, Luckley played in Hull Kingston Rovers 2025 Super League Grand Final victory over Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 19 February 2026, Luckley played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[World Club Challenge]] victory against [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/live/cwy6dlgx3kyt/|title=Relive Hull KR's World Club Challenge win over Brisbane|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
